Rainy Highway View.description: A view from inside a car driving on a wet highway at night, with rain streaking the windshield and headlights illuminating the road, creating a moody, atmospheric tone.

Person in Black Hooded Jacket.description: Wearing a black hooded jacket with 'Purdue' written in gold letters, walking through a wooded area with raindrops visible on the hood, accompanied by upbeat, slightly melancholic acoustic music.

News Headline on Phone.description: A phone screen displaying a news headline: 'Yosemite Closes After Wind Damage,' with the FOX 5 logo and 'SAN DIEGO HEADLINES' text, indicating a real-time update affecting travel plans.

YouTube Interface.description: A screen displaying a YouTube channel with various video thumbnails, including one titled 'The Newest Member of Yes Theory,' which the young man mentions as part of his backstory.

Campsite.description: A sunny forest campsite with a person in a blue shirt cooking over a campfire, surrounded by camping gear, including a trash can and a cooler, indicating a prepared outdoor setting.

Car Interior View.description: Shown from the passenger side, featuring a person in a gray jacket driving, with a map on the dashboard and a rainy highway visible through the windshield.

Person in Gray Jacket.description: Wearing a gray jacket, seen from behind while driving a car, with a map of Oregon visible on the dashboard, as the young man narrates about their journey through Oregon.

Map of Oregon.description: A physical map of Oregon, with a hand pointing to Crater Lake National Park, highlighting the travel route and destination.

Rocky Cliff Edge.description: A rugged, elevated rock formation with a forested mountain in the background, where the young man in the yellow hoodie stands, smiling at the camera under a clear blue sky.

Phone Screen.description: Showing a news headline: 'Yosemite Closes After Wind Damage,' with the FOX 5 logo and 'SAN DIEGO HEADLINES' text, which the young man's narration confirms as the reason for a previous trip's disappointment.

raw_segments: The video opens with a young man in a yellow hoodie and blue jacket speaking directly to the camera in a cold, forested area, reflecting on the journey becoming "serious" and how "tough this actually is." Upbeat, slightly melancholic acoustic music with male vocals begins as the scene quickly shifts to a blurry, fast-moving shot of a person in a black hooded Purdue jacket walking through a wooded area. The journey continues with a person in a gray jacket driving a car, a map of Oregon visible on the dashboard, while the young man narrates about the "unfortunate" weather they encountered at Crater Lake, a place he was "most excited for," but they tried to "end Oregon off on a high note." This narration overlays a breathtaking view of a misty, snow-covered mountain range and lake, where the young man stands on a rocky outcrop. A hand then points to Crater Lake National Park on the Oregon map, accompanied by the continuing acoustic music. The mood shifts to a car driving on a rainy highway at night, followed by a person in a red hoodie waving from the dimly lit back of the car, all set to the melancholic music. The young man's narration returns, stating that "After a full night of driving, we were able to finally enter Yosemite National Park," before promising "some backstory" over a bright, sunny campsite scene where a person cooks over a campfire. He explains he previously went on a trip with "Yes Theory" (shown via a YouTube interface thumbnail) and planned to go to Yosemite, but the "park was closed at the time," a fact reinforced by a phone screen displaying a news headline: 'Yosemite Closes After Wind Damage.' Driving through a snowy, forested mountain road, the young man in the yellow hoodie smiles at the camera, narrating that this trip to Yosemite "did mean a lot more" to him, feeling like "redemption" for not being able to enter the park last year. The minute concludes with a close-up of the young man's face, wind blowing his hair against a backdrop of a rocky cliff and forested mountain under a clear blue sky, as he begins to announce their arrival in Yosemite.

event_blocks.summary: A group of friends, guided by a plan to explore a TikTok-famous spot, embarked on a journey through Yosemite National Park, initially walking along a paved road bordered by pine trees and rocky terrain under a bright blue sky. A young man in an orange shirt was seen standing on a rocky outcrop, surveying the vast valley with its towering granite cliffs and dense forests, as the camera panned across the iconic landscape. The first-person perspective captured hiking through a sunlit meadow and ascending a steep, rocky trail. Upon reaching a powerful waterfall, one member exclaimed, "I'm getting soaked, my camera's getting soaked! Look, this shot is insane!" as the group got drenched and laughed amidst excited shouts. They then waded in a calm river before transitioning to a dimly lit interior where a hand turned a doorknob, followed by a narrator's voice stating, "After a long day, we decided to book an Airbnb, but we quickly realized that there was something a bit off," accompanied by an eerie, suspenseful sound effect.

Waterfall.description: A powerful, cascading waterfall surrounded by granite cliffs and greenery, with mist rising and people standing close to it, getting soaked, directly associated with the sounds of excited shouts and a hiker exclaiming about getting soaked and the 'insane' shot, emphasizing the raw natural beauty and physical interaction with the environment.

Group of Friends.description: Wearing hiking gear, including one in a blue backpack, seen walking along a road, climbing a rocky trail, and later wading in a river, and heard shouting 'Yo!' and 'Woo!' with excitement near the waterfall, showing their shared adventure and exhilaration.

First-Person Hiker View.description: A point-of-view shot of someone hiking through a sunlit meadow with tall grass and trees, then climbing a rocky path, providing an immersive perspective of the journey.

Hand Gripping Rock.description: A close-up of a hand firmly holding onto a rough, gray rock surface, suggesting a moment of physical effort or climbing, possibly during the ascent to the viewpoint.

Dimly Lit Door.description: A close-up of a hand turning a metallic doorknob in a dark, indoor setting, with a glimpse of a patterned floor, visually marking a transition from the outdoors, and accompanied by a narrator's voice explaining the decision to book an Airbnb and the realization that 'there was something a bit off,' followed by an eerie sound effect, setting a mysterious tone for the indoor space.

Young Man.description: Wearing an orange shirt and dark shorts, standing on a rocky outcrop overlooking a valley, later seen with wet hair and smiling near a waterfall, and heard exclaiming 'I'm getting soaked, my camera's getting soaked! This shot is insane!' while at the waterfall, indicating his direct interaction and excitement with the natural elements.

River.description: A calm, dark river with large boulders, where the group wades and stands, enjoying the water, surrounded by trees and stone walls, indicating a serene natural setting.

YOSEMITE NP.description: Displayed as white text with a red location pin over a blue sky, appearing over a scenic view of granite cliffs and forests, marking the location of the adventure.

raw_segments: The video opens with a male voice stating their plan to head into a spot they saw on TikTok in a tunnel, as two hikers walk along a paved road bordered by a stone wall, surrounded by pine trees and rocky terrain under a bright blue sky. Upbeat indie-pop music with male vocals begins to play, accompanying a close-up of a hand gripping a rock, followed by a young man in an orange shirt standing on a rocky outcrop, overlooking a vast valley with towering granite cliffs and dense forests. The camera pans across the breathtaking landscape, revealing the iconic granite domes and deep valleys of Yosemite National Park, with the text 'YOSEMITE NP' appearing on screen. The journey continues with a first-person view of someone hiking through a sunlit meadow, then climbing a steep, rocky trail with other hikers. The group reaches a powerful waterfall, where a male voice exclaims, "I'm getting soaked, my camera's getting soaked! Look, this shot is insane!" as they stand close to the cascading water, getting drenched and laughing, followed by excited shouts of "Yo!" and "Woo!". They then wade in a calm river, enjoying the refreshing water. The scene transitions from blurred trees to a dimly lit interior, where a hand turns a doorknob. A narrator's voice explains, "After a long day, we decided to book an Airbnb, but we quickly realized that there was something a bit off," concluding with an eerie, suspenseful sound effect.

event_blocks.summary: The video begins with a chaotic scene inside a dimly lit Airbnb, where a Group of Friends, including Chad, experience what they believe to be a haunting, evidenced by panicked shouts of "It's haunted!" and loud banging noises; a Young Man in a blue shirt describes the "haunted" Airbnb as he moves through the kitchen and opens a door to reveal a Shirtless Man. A black screen with the Text Overlay 'rip project west 2021-2021' marks a transition to Yosemite National Park, where the Young Man, now in a black t-shirt, recounts the previous night's "hectic" events. The Group of Friends, identified as Matt, Chad, Noah, Daniel, Nick, and the narrator, pose at the Yosemite National Park Sign, followed by the narrator explaining that the group split up for day two, with Matt, Chad, and Noah embarking on a "rigorous hike." The First-Person Vehicle View shows a hiking boot on the dashboard, indicating Daniel, Nick, and the narrator's exploration, culminating at the RAINBOW POOL DAY USE SITE Sign; a News Headline Overlay from the 'Modesto Bee' reports a 19-year-old's death, prompting the narrator to somberly note that it was a "really cool cliff jumping spot where someone actually ended up dying in the past year."

First-Person Vehicle View.description: A point-of-view shot from inside a moving vehicle, showing a foot in a hiking boot on the dashboard and a forested road passing by, suggesting travel or transit.

RAINBOW POOL DAY USE SITE Sign.description: A weathered, dark green sign for a day-use site, visually accompanied by a news headline overlay from 'Modesto Bee' reporting a 19-year-old's death, which the narrator explicitly references as a "cliff jumping spot where someone actually ended up dying in the past year."

Text Overlay 'rip project west 2021-2021.description: White text on a black screen, indicating a symbolic conclusion or reflection on a project or journey that lasted only one year.

Yosemite National Park Sign.description: A large, rectangular sign with a painted mountain landscape and the text 'Yosemite National Park' in bold white letters, mounted on a stone base, with the group of friends sitting on top.

Group of Friends.description: A group of at least six individuals, including the narrator, Matt, Chad, Noah, Daniel, and Nick, who are seen posing atop the 'Yosemite National Park' sign and are described by the narrator as having split into two groups for hiking and exploring.

News Headline Overlay.description: A white text box from 'Modesto Bee' reporting '19-year-old Antioch man dies at popular swimming and diving spot in Tuolumne County', with a small image of the Rainbow Pool area.

Shirtless Man.description: A man with curly hair, shirtless and wearing black shorts, standing in a kitchen holding a white object, possibly a phone or remote, in a dimly lit room.

Young Man.description: The primary narrator, first heard in a panicked, excited tone describing a "haunted" Airbnb, then in a calmer, reflective voice recounting a "hectic" night and the group's activities in Yosemite, identifying himself as one of the "Daniel, Nick, and I" group who explored, while "Matt, Chad, and Noah" hiked. He is visually seen wearing a black cap and blue shirt with a yellow 'M' logo in the haunted house, and later a black t-shirt with a backpack in the forest.

2008 Honda Odyssey.description: Implied by the interior view from a moving vehicle, with a foot in a hiking boot resting on the dashboard, driving through a forested road.

raw_segments: The video opens with a dark, shaky interior shot, accompanied by multiple panicked male voices shouting "It's haunted!" and "This place is haunted, Chad!", amidst sounds of commotion and banging. The scene transitions to a young man in a black cap and blue shirt with a yellow 'M' logo, who, in an excited and slightly panicked voice, explains they are in a "haunted" Airbnb. He moves quickly through a dimly lit home, passing a kitchen with cooking pots, before opening a door to reveal a shirtless man in black shorts holding a white object. The chaotic audio intensifies with screams, loud thuds, and frantic shouts of "Chad!" and "What the heck?!", suggesting a terrifying encounter. A black screen with white text 'rip project west 2021-2021' then appears, followed by a shift to bright, sunny outdoor scenes in Yosemite. The young man, now in a black t-shirt with a backpack, is seen walking through a forest, while the narrator, in a calmer, reflective tone, explains that "last night was hectic" due to the "haunted" experience. A group of five friends, including Matt, Chad, Noah, Daniel, Nick, and the narrator, are shown posing atop the 'Yosemite National Park' sign, as a text overlay reads 'pictures from their hike:'. The narrator details how the group split up for day two, with Matt, Chad, and Noah doing a "rigorous hike" while Daniel, Nick, and he explored. Scenic shots of a lone figure on a rocky peak and a first-person view from a moving vehicle with a hiking boot on the dashboard illustrate their journey. The segment concludes with a close-up of a weathered 'RAINBOW POOL DAY USE SITE' sign, overlaid with a 'Modesto Bee' news headline reporting a 19-year-old's death, which the narrator somberly references as a "really cool cliff jumping spot where someone actually ended up dying in the past year."

event_blocks.summary: A group of friends embarks on an adventurous journey, beginning with exhilarating cliff jumping into a river, captured with excited shouts and splashes. The narrative then shifts to travel, with a 2008 Honda Odyssey navigating scenic routes, including a drive towards Sequoia, indicated by roadside signs and repeated mentions of the destination. The group visits the General Sherman Tree, marveling at its immense size, before a nighttime casino visit where they test their luck. The journey continues through desert landscapes towards Zion National Park, marked by a 'Welcome to Utah' sign and a hike on a sandy trail, with one young man reflecting on their experiences after weeks on the road. The trip is punctuated by energetic music, shifts in environment from forests and cliffs to deserts and city lights, and moments of communication via walkie-talkie, all under the overarching theme of 'PROJECT WEST' and culminating in dramatic visual elements like explosions.

Car Interior View.description: Shown from the passenger side, with a young man smiling and looking out the window as the car drives through a forest, and later a side mirror reflecting the passing trees, while a male voice repeats 'To Sequoia,' and a different male voice says, 'I need more energy, I need more energy,' followed by another exclaiming, 'Just Sequoia!'

Cliff Jumping Scene.description: A rocky cliffside overlooking a pool, where individuals leap into the water, creating splashes, with others watching from the rocks, is filled with the sounds of splashing water, excited shouts like 'Yo, that is insane!' and 'This is gonna be awesome!', and an encouraging 'Go in!'.

Bedroom Scene.description: A dimly lit room with a bed, where shirtless young men are jumping and moving energetically, is filled with the sound of multiple voices intensely chanting 'MEGA BET!' repeatedly, alongside loud shouts and cheers, suggesting a party or celebration.

Group of Friends.description: Four young men standing together at the Utah state sign, dressed in casual hiking attire, celebrating their arrival with raised arms and smiles.

PROJECT WEST.description: Displayed as bold yellow text on a black background, introducing the project or journey.

River Pool.description: A deep, clear pool of water surrounded by rocky cliffs and trees, where people are seen jumping and swimming, is accompanied by the sounds of splashing water and excited shouts, including an encouraging 'Go in!' from one of the participants.

Young Man.description: Shirtless, muscular, and wearing black swim trunks, seen moving rapidly in a kitchen, indicating a transition from rest to activity, as a slightly muffled male voice begins a monologue, stating, 'So you may have noticed, but I'm looking very homeless right now. And look at this stash. I've never had it this much, so...'

Explosion.description: Appears twice in black-and-white, first as a large water explosion and later as a more intense mushroom cloud, possibly symbolizing a dramatic or climactic event.

Forest Trail.description: A narrow, sun-dappled path surrounded by tall pine trees and green underbrush, where a person in a gray hoodie walks, suggesting the start of an outdoor adventure, as an upbeat pop-rock song with male vocals plays.

Walkie-Talkie.description: A black handheld communication device, shown being held and used by multiple individuals, indicating coordination or communication during the journey.

Liquor & Sporting Goods Sign.description: A roadside sign with bold white text on a brown background, advertising 'Liquor & Sporting Goods' and 'In-N-Out Burger,' indicating a stop or destination.

Young Man in Pink Shirt.description: Wearing a pink t-shirt, seen from behind walking along a paved path in a forested area, with a wooden fence and trees in the background.

DAY 10 Text Overlay.description: White text displayed on screen, marking the tenth day of the journey, adding a narrative timeline to the video.

2008 Honda Odyssey.description: Implied by the interior view from a moving vehicle, with a foot in a hiking boot resting on the dashboard, driving through a forested road.

Young Man in Gray Shirt.description: Wearing a gray 'Michigan Basketball' t-shirt, handling a walkie-talkie, reinforcing the theme of communication.

He narrates their journey, detailing a casino pit stop where they had a 'successful night' despite losing 'like 20 bucks,' and outlines plans for a 'nonstop' hike in Zion National Park, which they enter 'with empty pockets' after 'multiple weeks of being on the road.'

General Sherman Tree.description: A massive sequoia tree, implied to be the largest by volume, with a detailed informational sign describing its height, volume, and age.

Informational Sign.description: A blue and white sign titled 'The General Sherman Tree,' detailing its dimensions and world records, with a hand pointing to specific data points.

Sandy Trail.description: A narrow, dusty path through a rocky, arid landscape with sparse vegetation, where the group hikes, with close-ups on their boots and legs moving through the sand.

Welcome to Utah Sign.description: A large roadside sign with 'Welcome to UTAH' in bold white letters and 'LIFE ELEVATED' below, featuring a red rock landscape graphic, where a group of friends poses with raised arms.

Young Man in Red Shirt.description: Wearing a red t-shirt, seen looking up at a massive tree with an expression of awe, then pointing to an informational sign about the General Sherman Tree.

raw_segments: The video opens with a serene scene of a shirtless young man wading through a calm, clear river, surrounded by large rocks and dense forest, with towering granite cliffs in the background under a bright blue sky. A male voiceover reflects, "creepy, but after debating it for a while, we decided to give it a go and I'm so glad that we did." The scene transitions to a close-up of a young man smiling in a car, looking out the window as the vehicle drives through a sun-dappled forested area. The voiceover continues, "Alright, so we're currently at a cliff jumping spot. It looks so beautiful." Upbeat, energetic music begins as the next sequence shows a group of people at a rocky cliffside, where one person jumps into a deep pool below, creating a large splash, prompting an excited "Yo, that is insane!" and "This is gonna be awesome!" from the group. The camera captures a high-angle shot of another person leaping from a cliff into the water, arms outstretched, with sunlight glinting off the surface, accompanied by splashing sounds and excited shouts, including a loud "WOO!" and an encouraging "Go in!". The scene shifts to a close-up of a smiling young man with wet hair, standing near the water, followed by a quick shot of a car driving through a forest, with the side mirror reflecting the passing trees. The video then cuts to a black-and-white explosion over water, resembling a nuclear test, with a small sun emoji in the corner. This is followed by a title card reading 'PROJECT WEST' in bold yellow letters. The male voiceover returns, stating, "This is our last day in Yosemite. I couldn't think of a better way to end it off. Spirits were high as we ended off the day and we knew that there was only one way that we could celebrate." This leads into a chaotic indoor scene where several shirtless young men are energetically jumping on a bed in a dimly lit room, with one wearing a black cap and another in a gray t-shirt, all intensely chanting "MEGA BET!" repeatedly and with increasing fervor, accompanied by loud shouts and cheers. The final frame shows a black-and-white explosion again, this time with a more intense mushroom cloud, reinforcing the dramatic climax as the chanting and shouts reach a crescendo.

The segment opens with a chaotic, energetic scene of shirtless young men jumping on a bed in a dimly lit bedroom, perfectly synchronized with loud, energetic music featuring distorted vocals and shouts. The camera quickly shifts to a kitchen, where a shirtless, muscular young man moves rapidly as the music abruptly fades, replaced by a slightly muffled male voice speaking. This voice continues a monologue, stating, "So you may have noticed, but I'm looking very homeless right now. And look at this stash. I've never had it this much, so..." The video then shows a close-up of a hand holding a black walkie-talkie, followed by a young man in a yellow shirt using it, then a man in a pink shirt preparing food, and another in a gray 'Michigan Basketball' shirt handling a walkie-talkie, reinforcing the theme of communication. An upbeat pop-rock song with male vocals and lyrics like "Hey baby, won't you look my way? I can be your new addiction" begins as the video transitions to an outdoor setting. Here, two young men, one in a pink shirt and the other in a navy 'Our Galaxy' shirt with a baseball cap, stand smiling in front of a garage door. The scene shifts to an aerial view of a silver minivan navigating a winding road through a dry, hilly landscape, with a male voice interjecting "To Sequoia" over the music. The camera then moves to a first-person perspective from inside the vehicle, driving along a forested road, before cutting to a roadside sign for 'Liquor & Sporting Goods' and 'In-N-Out Burger.' The male voice repeats "To Sequoia," followed by a different male voice saying, "I need more energy, I need more energy," and another exclaiming, "Just Sequoia!" The pop-rock song swells again with lyrics like "with the whisper, and I was when I kissed her, and then she made my lips hurt. I gave you the cha-cha. Take me to your love shack," as the final shot shows a person in a gray hoodie walking through a sun-dappled forested area, suggesting the start of an outdoor adventure. The segment is marked by dynamic movement, shifting environments, and a focus on communication and travel, with on-screen text 'DAY 10' indicating the progression of a journey.

The video opens with a dynamic, fast-moving shot from inside a vehicle driving through a dense forest, set to upbeat pop music with female vocals singing about "Mama's always got a backtrack." The scene transitions to a close-up of a young man in a red shirt looking up in awe at a massive tree, likely the General Sherman Tree, as he later points to its informational sign. Male voices are heard discussing the tree's size, with one asking, "Are you impressed?" and another noting its "lot more girth," followed by comments like "I don't care how fat it is" and a clarification that it's "not the tallest tree," but "unnamed," before someone asks, "Dan, what do you think?" The scene then shifts to a serene sunset over a lake with silhouetted mountains, transitioning to a nighttime drive through a city with bright lights, as the upbeat pop music returns with lyrics about being "my drug" and "my new prescription." A brief glimpse of a young man in a yellow shirt, seen at night in a parking lot, speaks directly to the camera, narrating their journey: "As we headed out of California, we thought it'd be fun to take a pit stop at a casino. With dreams of leaving this trip with both memories and a load of cash, we decided to test our luck and surprisingly, we actually made... successful night at the casino. Lost like 20 bucks, but it's all right. We got 7 hours to Zion now, gonna do a hike in the morning. Nonstop. We'll see how it goes." The journey continues with a drive through a desert landscape, set to serene, ambient music featuring piano and strings, culminating in a group of friends posing triumphantly at the 'Welcome to Utah' sign. The final scenes show the group hiking on a sandy trail through a rocky, arid environment, with close-ups of their boots and legs emphasizing the physical effort of the trek, as the young man in yellow shirt narrates, "So with empty pockets, we headed into Zion National Park. After multiple weeks of being on the road, I'd learned a lot." The atmosphere evolves from awe-inspiring natural beauty to adventurous exploration, with lighting shifting from bright daylight to twilight and then to nighttime city lights before returning to daylight in the desert.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with a first-person perspective of hikers, including a young man with a black backpack and blue wristband, ascending a rugged, rocky desert canyon trail, with close-ups on boots and hands gripping sandstone. The young man narrates reflections on the challenges of keeping a van clean with six people, the discomfort of sleeping in a Walmart parking lot, and the country's beauty. Reaching a dramatic canyon viewpoint, he gestures below while detailing their 'famously treacherous' 6,000-foot hike of Angel's Landing in Zion, describing it as 'difficult but also extremely rewarding,' which made them feel 'truly on top of the world.' The scene abruptly shifts to a roadside parking lot where the same young man, now shirtless and in black swim trunks, stands distressed near a silver minivan with a flat rear tire and a yellow jack, exclaiming, "Oh my god!" and asking, "Who did this?" Another voice is heard saying, "I knew we weren't allowed to park here." The young man's narration resumes, stating they are "currently down bad after our Angel's Landing hike." The video cuts to a black screen with an inset image of a man in jeans and a black t-shirt near the car, followed by a wide sunset view over a forested landscape. The young man concludes his narration, explaining they "ended up getting booted, unfortunately," and after "arguing extensively with the parking lot owner" and "even getting the cops involved," they left Zion that night with "200 less dollars."

Yellow Jack.description: A bright yellow car jack positioned under the silver minivan’s rear axle, used to lift the vehicle for tire replacement.

Young Man.description: Shirtless, wearing black swim trunks and a black backpack, with a blue wristband, actively hiking a steep, rocky trail and later seen near a flat-tired silver minivan in a parking lot, appearing distressed. He is the primary narrator, reflecting on the trip's challenges (van cleanliness, sleeping in a Walmart parking lot, flat tire, getting booted, arguing with parking lot owner, involving cops, losing money) and expressing frustration with an audible 'Oh my god!'.

Flat Tire.description: A deflated rear tire on the silver minivan, with a yellow jack supporting the vehicle. It is the immediate visual cause of distress in the parking lot, contributing to the group being 'down bad' as described by the narrator.

The narrator identifies it as a 'Walmart parking lot' where they slept, and later the location where they 'argued extensively with the parking lot owner' and 'got the cops involved' after getting 'booted'.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with a close-up of a United States map highlighting Utah and a 'Zion National Park' pin, accompanied by faint atmospheric music and a voice saying "pocket." Inside a 2008 Honda Odyssey, a Young Man (Narrator/Blue Tank Top) announces they are hiking the Narrows, while another in a gray cap looks down, leading to a humorous exchange about "nipple hair" and a search for water. The group then hikes a sun-dappled trail in a canyon with red rock walls and green foliage. The Young Man (Narrator/Blue Tank Top) explains they decided to go to Lake Powell in Arizona instead of continuing in Zion. At Lake Powell, marked by a sign, the group wades into the turquoise water, splashing and enjoying the coolness to upbeat rock music. A Young Man in Patterned Swim Trunks jumps in, creating a splash. Later, walking along a sandy shore, one carrying a backpack and another barefoot, the narrator states the trip was "well needed." The scene shifts to a long highway and a gas station where two men refuel. An excited voice asks, "Next stop?" followed by "Canyonlands!" and a collective "Woo!" Upbeat rock music returns. The video cuts to a nighttime scene, where a Young Man holds up a phone showing '12:20', and a Young Man in Beanie (Night) with a 'Zion National Park' logo appears. The Young Man in Blue Tank Top (Night) explains they are in Canyonlands, found a campsite after driving all day, and are tired. The Young Man in Beanie (Night) adds it's time to rest, noting the place is "sweet" and he "got to create the middle."

He contributes to the nighttime conversation with a male voice, agreeing about the need for rest and commenting on the campsite.

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of a map of the western United States, highlighting Utah and featuring a 'Zion National Park' pin, as faint, atmospheric music begins. A male voice is heard saying "pocket," followed by another exclaiming "Got it." Inside a moving vehicle, a young man in a blue tank top smiles at the camera, announcing, "Good morning everyone, we're hiking the Narrows today," while another in a gray cap looks down. A humorous exchange follows with one asking "You what?" and another responding, "Achieved my nipple hair," met with laughter. Someone then asks, "Where's another gallon of water?" to which another replies, "I don't know." The scene transitions to the group hiking along a rocky, sun-dappled trail in a canyon with towering red rock walls and green foliage. A male narrator explains, "Honestly, the second day in Zion wasn't as eventful as the first, so we decided to call an audible and head to Lake Powell in Arizona." The journey continues to Lake Powell, marked by a large white text overlay, where the group is seen wading into the turquoise water, splashing and enjoying the coolness, accompanied by upbeat, energetic rock music. A man in patterned swim trunks stands at the water’s edge, then jumps in, creating a splash, as someone exclaims "Stocky heart." The group is later seen walking along a sandy shore, one carrying a backpack and another walking barefoot, as the narrator states, "Alright, we're headed back now, but that was well needed." The scene shifts to a long, straight highway with a bridge in the distance, followed by a gas station where two men are refueling. An excited voice asks, "Next stop?" and another enthusiastically shouts, "Canyonlands!" followed by a collective "Woo!" Upbeat rock music returns, underscoring the continued journey. The video then cuts to a nighttime scene, where a young man holds up a phone showing the time '12:20', and another in a beanie with a 'Zion National Park' logo appears beside him, both illuminated by a headlamp. The young man in the blue tank top speaks to the camera in the dark, with his beanie-wearing friend beside him, explaining, "Alright, so it's currently midnight. We're in Canyonlands right now, and we found a campsite luckily. We've been driving all day, it was a pretty good day, but really tiring." His friend adds, "Yeah, it's, it's time to get some rest. This place is sweet though. I basically got to create the middle," suggesting they are preparing for a night hike or adventure after a long day.

event_blocks.summary: A blurry, dark shot of a full moon against a stormy sky transitions to a bright, sunlit desert landscape in Canyonlands, where a shirtless young man in black swim trunks and sandals climbs and crawls over large sandstone rocks, with voices commenting on the scenery and the trip's challenges. Upbeat, melancholic instrumental music begins as a narrator reflects on Canyonlands being a turning point, admitting fatigue and a desire to go home, while the young man laughs at the camera, prompting a dramatic plea for help from a voice claiming to be trapped with no signal or water, leading to commentary about him 'losing his mind.' The narrator notes the widespread fatigue and desire to leave, with friends visible in the background. The scene shifts to a low-angle shot of two young men looking at the sky, discussing plane tickets to fly home, with names like Nick, Noah, and Dan wanting to go home. The narrative then shifts to a young man in black swim trunks sitting on a sunlit sandstone rock in Canyonlands, with a narrator explaining their decision to 'tough out the rest of this trip' and head to Moab. The young man lies on his stomach, laughing at the camera, then crawls over rocks, accompanied by upbeat, folksy acoustic guitar music. A hand points to a map of the western United States with Zion and Canyonlands National Park pins. A voice greets, "Good morning from the Canyonlands," and later observes, "This is pretty cool." Upbeat, slightly melancholic instrumental music with a driving beat begins. A reflective male voice narrates that "Canyonlands was definitely a big turning point for us in this trip. We were tired, it was hot, and to be honest, a lot of us just wanted to go home." The camera follows the young man's movements closely, capturing his effort and determination. He is later seen lying on his stomach, laughing and smiling directly at the camera, with a white cloth or towel beside him, as a male voice dramatically pleads, "I've been trapped here for 18 days! The dramatic voice continues, "I have no cell signal. I have no water," before repeatedly yelling, "Please! Please!" The narrator adds that "A lot of people are feeling the effects of the fatigue. People want to go home. People want to skip things." Other friends are visible in the background, some sitting and relaxing on the rocks. The camera pans up to show a wide view of the expansive desert with distant rock formations under a clear blue sky. The scene shifts to a low-angle shot of two young men looking up at the sky, one shirtless and the other in a black tank top, both appearing relaxed and content, as multiple male voices converse about getting "plane tickets and fly home." The video ends with a wide shot of one of the young men lying on his back on the rock, legs bent, enjoying the sun, with the vast desert landscape stretching out behind him.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with a close-up of a hand on a textured surface, transitioning to a low-angle view of a young man under dim, warm lighting, as a male voice remarks, "This bad right here." The perspective shifts to a first-person view from within a moving car passing through a tunnel, a digital sign overhead indicating 'SPEED LIMIT 50 MPH,' while the voice muses, "It's been so long. We're gonna have a good night, and we're gonna get up in the morning and drive 19 hours home. It's been a crazy adventure. We're all dying," punctuated by a laugh, before stating, "And in the morning, after three weeks of being on the road, we would finally start our journey back home." Subsequently, a young man in a yellow t-shirt is seen operating a drone on a rocky, forested hillside, the drone ascending over a lake to capture the landscape. The video then presents rapid clips labeled 'one final karaoke session,' depicting several young men in a car enthusiastically singing and laughing to an upbeat pop song, "See You Again," with audible lyrics like "when I see you again." The scene changes to a top-down perspective of a wooden table adorned with stickers such as 'keep it wholesome' and 'live;more.' A hand sorts through a stack of printed photos—group shots, landscapes, and journey snapshots—while a male narrator, accompanied by soft, reflective music, expresses, "I love these guys. They're my best friends and this trip was the best experience of my life." Text overlays echo his sentiment with phrases like 'i love these guys' and 'i urge you to cherish these moments' as he advises, "I urge you to cherish these moments, make those memories, and to always keep it wholesome." The concluding frames show the hand setting the photos down, revealing the stickers, as the narrator signs off with, "Thanks for watching."

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with a Young Man in a blue puffer jacket exiting a building and entering a white car, stating his intention to pack everything into the van for a 7,000-mile journey, accompanied by laughter and energetic gestures from companions, including a Man in a White Tank Top and a Man in a Blue Shirt. The group playfully sings along to a song, admitting they don't know the lyrics, before the scene shifts to a darker car interior where the Young Man reiterates the car's importance amidst cheers. The video then transitions to a split-screen format featuring 'THANKS FOR WATCHING!' text, showcasing brief clips of a Man in a Black Shirt with a Yellow 'M' Logo, a Shirtless Man in Black Shorts dancing, a Man in a Blue Shirt and White Pants dancing, a messy room, friends hiking in snow, a Man in a Brown Hoodie and Orange Beanie in a snowy parking lot, and a dimly lit room with a couch. The outro continues with a split-screen showing a blurry, warm-toned face and a Walmart storefront with a white car labeled 'Campsite for the night,' followed by clips of friends in a car, a person pinning Chicago on a United States map, a young man in a blue hoodie, a group hiking, and a hand pointing to Chicago on a map. As the energetic music fades to a piano melody, the split-screen resolves to a young man in a light blue t-shirt, seated in a gaming chair, who delivers a reflective monologue thanking viewers, calling the project 'one of the most difficult things I've ever done,' and announcing his decision not to return to school, opting instead to focus on posting long-form content, storytelling, and adventures. The video concludes with the same young man, now holding a microphone, expressing gratitude with a smile and a final 'Keep it wholesome' before ending with 'THANK YOU.' text on a black screen.
